courses.name: Cake Design, Decoration & Pastry Masterclass
courses.description: Eight-week advanced training in cake artistry and pastry creation for events, celebrations, and commercial bakeries.
learn: Sugar craft, piping, and decorative detailing
Professional presentation for celebration cakes and pastry displays
skills: Cake covering, trimming, stacking, and finishing
courses.requirement: Basic knowledge or prior exposure to the trade is required. Completion of a basic course or equivalent experience is recommended. Applicants must provide valid identification and complete the registration process.
courses.value: This course builds the decorative precision and production confidence needed for paid cake orders, bakery work, and event pastry presentation.

courses.name: Continental Culinary Arts - Nigerian & African Cuisine
courses.description: Practical culinary training covering Nigerian, African, and continental dishes with plating, nutrition, and modern kitchen methods.
learn: Nigerian, African, and continental menu preparation
Plating, nutrition basics, and modern culinary methods
skills: Menu execution across multiple cuisine styles
courses.requirement: Basic knowledge or prior exposure to the trade is required. Completion of a basic course or equivalent experience is recommended. Applicants must provide valid identification and complete the registration process.
courses.value: Students strengthen practical kitchen range, presentation standards, and menu confidence for catering, restaurant, and food business work.

courses.name: Professional Food Safety Compliance & Kitchen Hygiene (C&G Certified)
courses.certificate: City & Guilds, MPVTL Vocational Certificate of Completion
courses.description: Internationally certified food safety training covering HACCP, sanitation, contamination control, and professional kitchen compliance.
learn: HACCP principles and food safety standards
Contamination control and safe food handling
skills: Safe storage, preparation, and service control
courses.requirement: Prior training or demonstrable experience in the relevant field is required. Applicants must provide valid identification, submit supporting evidence where applicable, and attend assessment or interview.
courses.value: This course is valuable for food handlers, caterers, and kitchen teams that need credible safety practice and certification-backed compliance knowledge.

courses.name: Hair Styling & Hair Making Technology
courses.description: Three-month training in cutting, braiding, weaving, styling, coloring, and hair treatments for salon work or self-employment.
learn: Cutting, braiding, weaving, and styling techniques
Salon preparation, client handling, and finishing
skills: Sectioning, styling, and protective hair work
courses.requirement: No formal educational qualification required. Applicants must be at least 14, provide valid identification, complete registration, and show willingness to follow MPVTL rules. Ability to read and write in English is an advantage.
courses.value: Learners build usable salon skills for everyday styling services, beauty business growth, and hands-on client work.

courses.name: Professional Pedicure & Manicure Services
courses.description: Focused nail care training covering grooming, hygiene, polishing techniques, and classic to modern salon styles.
courses.requirement: No formal educational qualification required. Applicants must be at least 14, provide valid identification, complete registration, and show willingness to follow MPVTL rules. Ability to read and write in English is an advantage.
courses.value: Students gain a compact, service-ready skill set for paid nail care work in salons, spas, and mobile beauty service settings.

courses.name: CCTV Camera Installation Program
courses.description: Practical CCTV training in camera setup, cabling, DVR/NVR configuration, and surveillance system troubleshooting.
learn: Cabling, connectors, DVR and NVR setup
Surveillance system testing and fault tracing
skills: Troubleshooting signal, power, and storage issues
courses.requirement: Basic knowledge or prior exposure to the trade is required. Completion of a basic course or equivalent experience is recommended. Applicants must provide valid identification and complete the registration process.
courses.value: The course prepares learners to install, configure, and support CCTV systems for homes, offices, shops, and facilities.

courses.name: Computer Appreciation
courses.description: Beginner digital skills training covering typing, file management, internet use, email, and Microsoft Office tools.
learn: Keyboarding, file management, and computer navigation
Microsoft Office tools for work and study
courses.requirement: No formal educational qualification required. Applicants must be at least 14, provide valid identification, complete registration, and show willingness to follow MPVTL rules. Ability to read and write in English is an advantage.
courses.value: This course builds practical digital confidence for learners entering office work, business administration, study, or further ICT training.

courses.name: Computer Networking
courses.certificate: MPVTL Vocational Certificate of Completion, City & Guilds optional
courses.description: Hands-on networking course covering IP addressing, cabling, switching, routing, wireless networking, and security.
learn: IP addressing and basic network design
Cabling, switching, routing, and wireless setup
courses.requirement: Basic knowledge or prior exposure to the trade is required. Completion of a basic course or equivalent experience is recommended. Applicants must provide valid identification and complete the registration process.
courses.value: Students gain the practical network setup and troubleshooting skills needed for ICT support, office connectivity, and technical service roles.

courses.name: Cyber Security
courses.description: Cybersecurity training covering threat detection, ethical hacking basics, password security, and network defense strategies.
learn: Common cyber threats and attack patterns
Ethical hacking concepts and defensive thinking
Password, endpoint, and network protection practices

courses.requirement: Prior training or demonstrable experience in the relevant field is required. Applicants must provide valid identification, submit supporting evidence where applicable, and attend assessment or interview.
courses.value: This course strengthens defensive thinking and technical awareness for learners progressing into cybersecurity or ICT protection work.

courses.name: ICT Hardware and Software Maintenance
courses.certificate: MPVTL Vocational Certificate of Completion, City & Guilds optional
courses.description: Computer maintenance training in assembling, upgrading, repairing, and troubleshooting desktop and laptop systems.
skills: Fault diagnosis for common computer issues
courses.requirement: Basic knowledge or prior exposure to the trade is required. Completion of a basic course or equivalent experience is recommended. Applicants must provide valid identification and complete the registration process.
courses.value: Learners develop practical repair and maintenance ability for computer workshops, ICT departments, and independent support services.

courses.name: Foundational Data Science & Analytics
courses.description: Practical analytics training using Excel, Python, statistics, visualization, and business data analysis.
learn: Statistics, visualization, and business insight reporting
courses.requirement: Basic knowledge or prior exposure to the trade is required. Completion of a basic course or equivalent experience is recommended. Applicants must provide valid identification and complete the registration process.
courses.value: This course helps learners turn raw business information into charts, summaries, and useful decisions with modern analytics tools.

courses.name: Advanced Electrical Installation
courses.description: Advanced electrical installation training covering three-phase systems, motor controls, panel wiring, and industrial electrical systems.
courses.requirement: Prior training or demonstrable experience in the relevant field is required. Applicants must provide valid identification, submit supporting evidence where applicable, and attend assessment or interview.
courses.value: Experienced learners build higher-level competence for industrial installations, maintenance work, and supervisory electrical tasks.

courses.name: Beginner Electrical Installation
courses.description: Foundational electrical installation course covering domestic and industrial wiring, circuit design, distribution boards, testing, and troubleshooting.
skills: Safe use of electrical tools and testers
courses.requirement: No formal educational qualification required. Applicants must be at least 14, provide valid identification, complete registration, and show willingness to follow MPVTL rules. Ability to read and write in English is an advantage.
courses.value: Students gain the practical foundation needed to install and maintain safe electrical systems under proper supervision.

courses.name: Automobile Electrical Works
courses.description: Vehicle electrical training covering batteries, wiring, sensors, lighting circuits, diagnostics, ECU basics, and fault finding.
learn: Automotive batteries, wiring, and lighting circuits
Sensors, ECU basics, and diagnostic flow
skills: Diagnostic reporting for cars, buses, and heavy-duty vehicles
courses.requirement: Basic knowledge or prior exposure to the trade is required. Completion of a basic course or equivalent experience is recommended. Applicants must provide valid identification and complete the registration process.
courses.value: Learners build the confidence to service and repair modern vehicle electrical systems across common workshop situations.

courses.name: Mechatronics System Principles & Fault Finding (C&G Certified)
courses.certificate: City & Guilds, MPVTL Vocational Certificate of Completion
courses.description: City & Guilds mechatronics training in control systems, PLC basics, and fault finding for modern automated equipment.
courses.requirement: Prior training or demonstrable experience in the relevant field is required. Applicants must provide valid identification, submit supporting evidence where applicable, and attend assessment or interview.
courses.value: This course supports progression into automation and maintenance roles where mechanical, electrical, and control systems meet.

courses.name: Solar System Installation
courses.description: Solar installation training covering panel mounting, battery banks, inverters, charge controllers, system sizing, and troubleshooting.

courses.requirement: Basic knowledge or prior exposure to the trade is required. Completion of a basic course or equivalent experience is recommended. Applicants must provide valid identification and complete the registration process.
courses.value: Students gain practical renewable energy skills for installation companies, facility support, and independent solar service work.

courses.name: Manual Arc Welding Technology (SMAW)
courses.certificate: MPVTL Vocational Certificate of Completion, City & Guilds optional
courses.description: Shielded metal arc welding training focused on electrode handling, joint preparation, and strong clean welds for repairs and construction.

Clean weld production for repair and construction tasks
courses.requirement: No formal educational qualification required. Applicants must be at least 14, provide valid identification, complete registration, and show willingness to follow MPVTL rules. Ability to read and write in English is an advantage.
courses.value: Learners build the core welding control needed for repair work, construction support, and progression into fabrication.

courses.name: Welding & Metal Fabrication Technology
courses.certificate: MPVTL Vocational Certificate of Completion, City & Guilds optional
courses.description: Hands-on fabrication training in cutting, joining, structural assembly, workshop safety, blueprint reading, and practical welding techniques.
learn: Metal cutting, joining, and structural assembly
Fabrication of gates, tanks, frames, and industrial structures
skills: Assembly and finishing of metal projects
courses.requirement: Basic knowledge or prior exposure to the trade is required. Completion of a basic course or equivalent experience is recommended. Applicants must provide valid identification and complete the registration process.
courses.value: Students gain practical fabrication ability for construction, manufacturing, and engineering workshop projects.

courses.name: AutoCAD Training
courses.description: Computer-aided design and drafting training using AutoCAD for 2D drawings, basic 3D drawings, technical plans, and layout designs.

courses.requirement: Basic knowledge or prior exposure to the trade is required. Completion of a basic course or equivalent experience is recommended. Applicants must provide valid identification and complete the registration process.
courses.value: Learners build drafting skills used in engineering, architecture, construction, and technical project communication.

courses.name: Plumbing & Pipe Fitting
courses.description: Plumbing course covering domestic and industrial systems, pipe joining methods, drainage, water supply layouts, fittings, and maintenance.
courses.requirement: No formal educational qualification required. Applicants must be at least 14, provide valid identification, complete registration, and show willingness to follow MPVTL rules. Ability to read and write in English is an advantage.
courses.value: Students gain practical plumbing skills for construction sites, facility maintenance, and supervised installation work.

courses.name: Professional Masonry & Construction Technology
courses.description: Practical masonry and construction training covering bricklaying, concrete works, plastering, tiling, and site practice.

skills: Basic site measurement and material handling
courses.requirement: Basic knowledge or prior exposure to the trade is required. Completion of a basic course or equivalent experience is recommended. Applicants must provide valid identification and complete the registration process.
courses.value: Learners build construction-site competence for masonry, finishing, and practical building trade work.

courses.name: Teaching, Training & Assessing Learning (C&G Certified)
courses.certificate: MPVTL Vocational Certificate of Completion, City & Guilds optional
courses.description: Practical programme for professionals who need to teach, train, and assess vocational learners effectively.
learn: Assessment evidence, feedback, and record keeping
courses.requirement: Prior training or demonstrable experience in the relevant field is required. Applicants must provide valid identification, submit supporting evidence where applicable, and attend assessment or interview.
courses.value: This course helps experienced practitioners convert trade knowledge into structured teaching, assessment, and learner support practice.

courses.name: Trade Test Preparation
courses.certificate: MPVTL Vocational Certificate of Completion, Trade Test Certification
courses.description: Preparation course for engineering trade test certification through practical drills, refresher theory, mock assessment, and exam readiness training.
courses.requirement: Basic knowledge or prior exposure to the trade is required. Completion of a basic course or equivalent experience is recommended. Applicants must provide valid identification and complete the registration process.

courses.name: Engineering Health and Safety (C&G Certified)
courses.certificate: City & Guilds, MPVTL Vocational Certificate of Completion
courses.description: Engineering safety course covering hazard identification, basic risk assessment, and safe working procedures in technical environments.
learn: Safe working procedures and control measures
courses.requirement: Prior training or demonstrable experience in the relevant field is required. Applicants must provide valid identification, submit supporting evidence where applicable, and attend assessment or interview.

questions.questionText: Can you read and write in English?
Are you available for practical training?
